research_goal: Understand why PMs at early-stage startups struggle with discovery research.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Maya, I saw your note about replacing long research cycles with weekly customer conversations. To start, what makes discovery hardest for your team right now?

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: The hard part is not getting calls. We can get customers on the phone. The hard part is that every call creates a pile of notes and nobody has time to turn that into a better next call.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: When that follow-up work gets skipped, what breaks downstream?

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: We keep asking broad questions for too long. By the fourth call we should be testing a sharper hypothesis, but instead we are still asking people to tell us about their workflow from scratch.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: What usually prevents the team from sharpening those questions between calls?

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: Context is scattered. Sales notes live in the CRM, support has Slack threads, I have call notes in docs, and none of that is in front of me when I am writing the next interview guide.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: If an agent handled part of that loop, where would you still want control?

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: I would want to approve the direction, not rewrite every question. Show me what changed, why it changed, and what evidence it came from. If I trust that, I can move fast.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: What would make the output stakeholder-ready instead of just useful for you?

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: Executives need the pattern, the confidence level, and a few quotes. They do not want transcripts. They want to know what changed in our understanding and what decision it supports.

summary: Maya says early-stage PMs can schedule discovery calls but fail to turn each call into sharper follow-up questions. The main blockers are scattered context, no time for synthesis, and low trust in outputs unless the system shows what changed and why.
